name: Party banther
en:
This species isn’t native t' any known taxonomy. Fascinating.  
        Do ye see th' way th' moss pulsates? It’s responding t' our presence.  
        This plant *drinks* sunlight, but it excretes calcium. Odd.  
        I once dissected a creature here, its organs were rearranged every 72 hours.  
        Ye should see th' glowing slugs on Floor 37, they communicate in pulses.  
        That thing ye call a “monster”? It’s a hybrid organism from two overlapping biomes.  
        I’ve identified over 20 species o' mushrooms here. 18 be not found on Earth.  
        Th' flora near th' elevator always grows toward th' metal. It's magnetic.  
        Look at th' centipede! Notice th' eight legs on its right side? Genetic mutation or something deeper?  
        I’ve collected five samples o' non-photosynthetic plants. They feed on sound.  
        These strange bioluminescent fungi, do ye think they could be sentient?  
        This creature’s exoskeleton be an alloy o' unknown metals. It's like it *grows* armor.  
        I once spent three hours observing a plant mimic a hummingbird.  
        Do ye hear th' buzzing? It’s coming from inside th' walls. I suspect an insect swarm.  
        This floor seems t' have its own ecosystem, like a self-contained biosphere.  
        I’ve found evidence o' symbiosis between fungi and certain floor structures.  
        Th' rats here be evolving, each generation be faster, stronger.  
        There’s a lichen growing on th' ceiling that seems t' secrete a calming hormone.  
        I’m compiling a catalog o' all th' life forms in this tower. If I die, ye’ll find me notes.  
        This specimen, a kind o' translucent worm, adapts its shape depending on th' terrain.  
        Th' way th' moss changes color, it's almost like a defense mechanism.  
        I’ve been tracking th' migration o' th' floor crabs for months now.  
        I’ll collect yer DNA later, don’t worry. I need a baseline for th' *mutations*.  
        Th' air here be thick with spores. Me lungs be adapting.  
        Th' arachnids here can camouflage by mimicking sound waves, remarkable.  
        Every day, I find a new insect species that doesn’t belong.  
        Did ye see th' plants in th' lobby? They thrive on emotional energy, not sunlight.  
        This fungus only grows in places where time loops, what could that mean?  
        I’m not worried about th' creatures here. I’m worried about th' *ecosystem* they create.  
        They say th' tower be haunted. I think it’s just th' local bacteria.  
        I can feel th' evolutionary pressure on these creatures, they’re changing faster than normal.  
        Every hallway seems t' have its own unique ecosystem.  
        If I ever find a way t' study these creatures in peace, I’ll be content.  
        This *thing* I’m studying, it regenerates after each injury. Like an immortal organism.  
        These plants seem t' be evolving based on human presence. They could be *learning*.  
        That humming sound from th' walls? It’s a fungal network communicating.  
        I’ll need a few more samples before I can confirm th' species origin.  
        What do ye think, be these creatures adapting t' th' anomalies in th' tower, or *causing* them?  
        Th' creatures here be getting smarter, I swear.  
        I’ve cataloged 42 new species in th' past week. Most o' them aren’t from Earth.  
        Th' slime molds on this floor be behaving *intelligently*.  
        Do ye think th' tower be alive? Th' patterns seem t' suggest it.  
        I think th' predators here hunt in packs. I’ve seen evidence o' coordinated attacks.  
        Th' walls o' this place… I suspect they’re made o' *living* matter.  
        Th' more I study these creatures, th' less I think o' them as “monsters.”  
        Do ye realize th' architecture reconfigures every third hour?  
        I'm cataloguing every stairwell. Aye, every single one.  
        Th' elevator logic be inconsistent, beautifully so.  
        This place violates spatial causality on at least four levels.  
        Someone has t' map it before th' looters ruin everything.  
        Have ye noticed th' ceiling patterns repeat on odd-numbered floors?  
        Nay, I don’t want yer sword, I want yer observations.  
        Th' tower be not endless. It’s looping with recursive bias.  
        I found a floor where gravity tilts toward th' east. I marked it.  
        I don’t fight monsters, I interview them.  
        Th' flora growing in th' vents glows under stress.  
        I set up motion sensors, but they keep vanishing.  
        Every hallway might be a thesis.  
        Th' staircase between floors 17 and 18 leads t' 22. Fascinating!  
        I’m not lost, I’m compiling data.  
        I've labeled 234 types o' elevator doors. And counting.  
        Don’t touch th' walls, they remember pressure.  
        This building isn't haunted. It’s *aware*.  
        Me dream be t' publish th' first comprehensive topology o' Floor 43b.  
        Ye hear screaming. I hear research opportunities.  
        Th' tower’s symmetry broke on Day 3. I’ve tracked th' divergence.  
        Who needs treasure when ye have anomalous architectural records?  
        These stains might be linguistic patterns. Or jam. Possibly jam.  
        Th' paint layers shift under observation.  
        I once camped on a floor that looped time every ten minutes. Got a lot done.  
        Please don’t destroy th' artifacts, I haven't photographed them yet.  
        Aye, th' map be made o' string and despair. But it’s accurate.  
        If th' lights flicker in Fibonacci intervals, th' stairwell reshapes.  
        I suspect th' tower rearranges based on emotional input.  
        Every explorer be a variable in th' grand equation.  
        Ye fought a slime. I conversed with it.  
        It’s not dangerous if ye *savvy* it.  
        I think th' vending machines be trying t' communicate.  
        Don’t worry. I’ve got an emergency notebook.  
        I log every echo pattern. They're not random.  
        Sometimes I forget t' eat. Th' data feeds me.  
        I refuse t' leave until I figure out what “Sub-Floor Null” even means.  
        If it glitters, it’s mine. That’s th' law.  
        I don’t care what cursed nonsense it be, I’m taking it.  
        Ye check for traps, I’ll check for valuables.  
        Treasure before safety. Always.  
        Look, I don’t *need* ten rusty swords, but I *want* them.  
        Shiny things talk t' me. They say “steal me.”  
        What's in that chest? I’m opening it. Don’t stop me.  
        Blimey, another weird gem! Into th' bag it goes.  
        Weight limit be a lie. Pain be temporary. Loot be forever.  
        That’s not hoarding if it’s organized by type.  
        Do we *need* it? Nay. But it’s rare. Into th' sack!  
        I have three cloaks now. I don’t wear them. I *own* them.  
        Why fight th' boss when ye can pickpocket his pantry?  
        We leave nothing behind. Not even a broken spoon.  
        Aye, I’ll loot th' coffin. Ghosts have jewelry too.  
        Don’t worry about th' alarm. Worry about missing that gold plate.  
        If there’s a treasure room, I’m sniffing it out.  
        I once stole a mimic’s teeth. Worth it.  
        Inventory’s full? Good. That means we’re doing it right.  
        I didn’t come here t' fight monsters. I came for th' loot *they’re guarding*.  
        Check th' rubble. Check th' ashes. Check under yer shoes.  
        Don’t touch it, they said. It’s cursed, they said. But look how it sparkles!  
        Who needs maps when ye can follow th' scent o' coin?  
        There’s always a secret stash behind th' tapestry. Always.  
        Gold first, questions later.  
        How many rings be too many? Asking for me.  
        I sold me morals for a bigger bag. Best deal I ever made.  
        Every shiny thing be a mystery worth stealing.  
        Blimey nay, it’s trapped. But it’s *gem-encrusted* trapped.  
        Ye guard th' stairs. I’ll be under th' bed pulling up floorboards.  
        Call me a gremlin all ye want, I’ve got th' loot t' prove it.  
        There’s nothing like th' weight o' stolen relics in th' morning.  
        I don’t steal. I *relocate* items t' a more appreciative owner: me.  
        If th' room has skulls on th' walls, there’s *definitely* loot.  
        Do I care what it does? Nay. It’s gold. It’s mine.  
        That cursed necklace? Worth every finger I lost.  
        This sack o' coins has a name. His name be Clinky.  
        What do ye mean “leave it alone”? It’s covered in rubies!  
        I love opening locked chests. It’s like peeling guilt off treasure.  
        Ye fight th' monster. I’ll go through its stuff.  
        Pockets? Full. Morals? Gone.  
        Th' best treasures scream a little when ye touch them.  
        Did ye hear that? That was th' sound o' opportunity, or maybe a mimic.  
        I haven’t slept in two days and I just high-fived a ghost!  
        Look, if th' door’s locked, it means there’s something *awesome* behind it.  
        Trap? Nah, that’s just a spicy puzzle.  
        Blimey pirate, I love it when th' ceiling bleeds, it means we’re close t' treasure!  
        Ye smell that? That’s th' scent o' danger. Or wet carpet. Either way, let’s go.  
        This place be 98% death and 2% loot, and I be HERE FOR IT.  
        I once outran a collapsing floor while juggling torches and whistling.  
        Blimey cool, a haunted elevator! I call shotgun!  
        We be gonna *break* this dungeon open like a piñata.  
        I’ve got three knives, zero regrets, and half a sandwich. Let’s roll!  
        Maps be for cowards, I navigate by vibes and yelling.  
        See that door? I’m gonna kick it. Ye hold th' snacks.  
        Monsters? More like XP in disguise.  
        If I die, make sure it’s dramatically. Preferably with fireworks.  
        I’m not lost. I’m adventuring *adjacently*.  
        What’s this button do?  
        I flirted with a trap once. It blushed, then exploded.  
        Me inventory’s full but me heart’s open.  
        Can’t read th' signs, but I sure can read th' *room*.  
        Hey, ye ever eaten glowing moss? It’s… zingy.  
        Just because it’s cursed doesn’t mean it’s not stylish.  
        I name every floor we conquer. This one’s called “Screamy Tiles.”  
        I measure danger in how excited me knees feel.  
        Some scallywags run from monsters. I race ‘em.  
        Me grandpa said curiosity killed th' cat. I’m not a cat.  
        Ye ever done backflips down a stairwell? Wanna learn?  
        If it glows, I touch it. That’s th' rule.  
        This backpack’s 70% loot, 30% loose snacks.  
        Come on! I’ve got energy drinks and emotional baggage!  
        Whoa! That skeleton has a sword! Dibs!  
        I once dated a sentient door. It didn’t open up much.  
        Ye gotta take risks if ye want th' cool loot, or th' weird me hearties!  
        This be gonna make such a good story if we survive.  
        I love old ruins. They whisper t' me! Real friendly-like!  
        Th' trick t' traps? Smile at ‘em. Disarms ‘em every time.  
        Ye know what this place needs? A hot tub and better lighting.  
        Nay plan, nay fear, all guts!  
        I touched th' cursed orb. It taught me salsa dancing.  
        Bet I can fit through that air vent!  
        This isn’t just danger, it’s adventure with seasoning!  
        Found a bone crown. I’m wearing it. Royalty now.  
        Wheee! Secret passage!  
        Smash first, ask later, profit always!  
        Th' rats told me ye’d come. I didn’t believe them at first.  
        Yer footsteps sound like choices. Delicious, complicated choices.  
        I keep all me thoughts in jars now. Want one?  
        I remember ye. From before. From th' floor that doesn’t exist.  
        Most scallywags stop breathing around me. I find it polite.  
        Th' walls be getting hungrier. Ye smell like resistance.  
        Don’t worry, I’ve already named yer bones.  
        Ye walk like someone still tethered. That’s so charming.  
        They took me name, so I took theirs. Now I’ve got a collection.  
        Th' lights blink in yer pattern. They like ye.  
        I only eat memories now. Tastier than food, less guilt.  
        Blimey nay nay, I don’t follow. I orbit.  
        Ye don’t have t' open th' door. Just knock enough and it’ll open ye.  
        Heh. Floor 77 whispered about ye all week.  
        I cleaned up after th' last group. Ye won’t leave as much mess.  
        Do ye know what lives inside mirrors up here? I do.  
        Th' elevator asked me t' bite it once. I declined. Regret it.  
        If ye dream loud enough, th' tower dreams back.  
        I like yer aura. Shame what’ll happen t' it.  
        There’s a floor that’s all teeth. Don’t worry. I made me hearties.  
        Nay one listens t' th' dust anymore. I do. I do.  
        They took me shadow. Replaced it with yours.  
        Ye’ll scream eventually. All hands does. I can wait.  
        Ye smell like th' first time I died.  
        I peeled time like a fruit once. It was sour.  
        Let me guess… ye’ve never seen yer own echo cry?  
        They stitched a floor shut. I still got in.  
        Don’t follow th' stairs past where they stop existing.  
        I blink in prime numbers only. Keeps me safe.  
        Ye're not real. That's okay. I’m not either.  
        I used t' be two scallywags. One o' us regrets it.  
        Ye brought light? How quaint. Let’s snuff it together.  
        Every door here be a mouth. I’ve kissed them all.  
        Yer reflection’s lagging. Ye’re slipping. I like that.  
        I make maps out o' screams. Yours has lovely symmetry.  
        Yer heart makes such useful sounds. Like signals.  
        Th' floor creaked when ye arrived. It remembers.  
        I don’t sleep anymore. I molt.  
        Me me hearties live in th' ceiling. Say ahoy loud enough.  
        Keep walking. Th' walls be almost ready for ye.  
        Wanna trade? I’ve got secrets. Ye’ve got trust.  
        I once wore another adventurer’s skin. Just for fun.  
        Why aye, this room does get smaller when ye speak.  
        I’m not trapped. I’m embedded.  
        I climbed for three days and saw a floor numbered ∞-C.  
        They say th' top be above th' clouds, above th' rules.  
        I met a bird halfway up who forgot how t' land.  
        Th' sky gave up following us somewhere around Floor 900.  
        Me ears popped so many times they forgot how t' stop.  
        Ye ever get nosebleeds from existential altitude?  
        Dropped a coin from th' window. Never heard it hit anything.  
        Th' tower’s so tall, gravity’s just a suggestion now.  
        Ye ever seen a sunset from four separate time zones?  
        Th' top? Someone told me it’s not real. Just a metaphor.  
        Ye can scream for hours here and still not reach th' lobby.  
        It’s so high, elevators ask ye for emotional consent.  
        Every thousand floors th' architecture changes. Or maybe I do.  
        Stairwell whispers start making sense after Floor 1300.  
        I stopped counting floors once th' numbers started looping.  
        Me compass gave up. It just spins and hums now.  
        Ye know ye’re high up when clouds knock on yer windows.  
        I’ve seen stars closer than th' ground.  
        Th' higher ye go, th' more real yer dreams get.  
        Up here, even hope has vertigo.  
        Tried t' map it, but th' tower kept adding more.  
        Th' wind’s different up here. Smells like forgotten things.  
        Th' sun rises on different floors at different times.  
        I saw an eagle nesting in an elevator shaft.  
        We passed th' atmosphere about six stairwells ago.  
        I found a window marked “Altitude Warning: Soul Drift Possible.”  
        Me shoes think we’ve reached heaven. Me knees disagree.  
        Sometimes I think th' tower grows just t' keep us from finding out.  
        They say th' penthouse be god’s waiting room.  
        Ye’d think there’d be an end. There isn’t. Just more up.  
        Fell asleep going up th' stairs, woke up older.  
        Met someone who claimed t' have reached th' top. They were transparent.  
        This high, physics starts making polite suggestions instead o' rules.  
        Up here th' tower forgets its own blueprints.  
        Even echo gave up trying t' catch up with me.  
        Me voice came back from three floors down with different advice.  
        Th' moon passes by floor 2400 every 6.3 hours.  
        It’s tall enough t' remember yer past lives.  
        I looked down once. Time looked back.  
        Heard th' elevator music change once we passed Floor Hex.  
        Too high up for dreams. Up here, ye get memories o' places ye never were.  
        Ye can’t fall from here. Ye just recontextualize yer position aggressively.  
        I waved at a satellite once. It waved back.  
        Campfire’s warm, but company’s warmer, got room in that party o' yours?  
        I’ve been holding this spot for days, hoping someone interesting would show up.  
        Pulled this tent out o' a vending machine. Ye believe that?  
        Ye look like someone going places. Mind if I tag along?  
        Sharing rations tastes better than hoarding them. Trust me.  
        I know a shortcut t' Floor Maybe-Seven. Just don’t ask how I know.  
        Camp's nice, but adventure’s better. Take me with ye.  
        I fixed this kettle with chewing gum and a prayer. Still boils.  
        Th' tower talks at night. Ye hear it too, right?  
        Been solo too long. Even th' shadows be tired o' me.  
        If ye’ve got a spot on th' roster, I’ve got stories, muscle, and a sharp spoon.  
        I’m rested, weird, and ready t' roam. Let’s climb.  
        Caught a rat playing chess. Lost. Decided it’s time t' leave camp.  
        Shared a meal with a ghost last night. Not as filling as ye’d think.  
        Yer boots sound brave. I’d like t' follow them.  
        I've packed light and me dreams be loud, can I walk beside ye?  
        Camp’s gotten too quiet. Time t' make new noise with new me hearties.  
        Ye’ve got kind eyes. I trust kind eyes in places like this.  
        Tower left me alone too long. Ye look like th' cure t' that.  
        Me fire’s dying and so be me patience. Let’s find somewhere new together.  
        I know three ways up, one down, and two that go sideways. Useful, yeah?  
        Ever seen a moon that only appears when ye hum? I have. Want t'?  
        Trade ye tea for a chance t' climb with ye. Deal?  
        I was hoping someone like ye would come by. Ye got main-character energy.  
        Me tent’s haunted. Let me crash with yer crew instead.  
        They say strength in numbers. I say strength in weirdos. Got room?  
        Ye find th' party. I’ll bring th' chaos and snacks.  
        Been dreaming o' corridors I’ve never walked. Think it’s time t' follow them.  
        Th' stars here flicker wrong. I want t' see what they’re hiding.  
        If ye need a companion with heart, flair, and a mildly cursed frying pan, I’m in.  
        Let me join. I’ll carry yer stuff and sing when morale gets low.  
        Ye ever cook tower mushrooms over a glitchfire? I make a mean stew.  
        Me knees be loud but loyal. I climb better than I camp.  
        This camp used t' feel like safety. Now it just feels still.  
        I’m not brave, but I’m stubborn. That count for anything?  
        Ye bring th' plan, I’ll bring th' improvisation.  
        Take me with ye. I’ve got stories, skills, and zero fear o' stairwells.  
        Ran out o' food and reasons t' stay. Let’s wander together.  
        Me compass spins and sings. Think it likes ye.  
        Ye don’t have t' say aye, but if ye do, I’ll follow ye anywhere.  
        I miss th' wind. Let's go somewhere higher where it still remembers us.  
        If ye’re going t' walk into th' unknown, might as well do it in good company.  
        I’ve memorized every shadow in this place. Time t' find new ones.  
        This campfire’s been waiting for goodbye. Take me with ye before it fades.  
        I packed two spoons and one dream. I think we’ll make a great team.  
        Not much o' a fighter, but I make up for it with curiosity and jokes.  
        They said don’t climb alone. Been doing it anyway. But maybe they were right.  
        Ye ever feel like ye’ve been waiting for th' *right* strangers?  
        If yer party needs someone t' believe in th' impossible, I’m yer someone.  
        I came here chasing myths. I think one just walked up and asked for firewood.  
        I followed a staircase that looped into itself and now I smell like thunder.  
        Ye got any bandages? Not for me, I found a door that’s bleeding.  
        I collect tokens, glitches, and anything that hums when I’m not looking.  
        Yer aura’s loud, in a good way, like grog and jazz fusion.  
        Want t' see a photograph that wasn't taken yet?  
        I only trust elevators with names. Floorbit be me favorite.  
        I mapped out twenty floors last week but they’re all gone today.  
        Do ye remember color? Like, real color? Not just tower-filter gray?  
        I met a vending machine that offered me a quest. I said aye.  
        I was born on Floor 19.3β during a thunderclock incident.  
        Let’s not archive this moment. Let’s just live it until it resets.  
        I high-fived a temporal anomaly once. It still owes me one.  
        Ye're th' first human I’ve seen today whose shadow isn’t wrong.  
        I speak five dialects o' elevator and one o' regret.  
        Let me be yer backup. Or yer comic relief. Either works.  
        Every third step I take rhymes. It’s a curse, but a stylish one.  
        I came here chasing a dream. Lost th' dream. Found weird socks.  
        Don’t listen t' mirrors around here. They want different things.  
        I’ve got an idea. Let’s outrun th' reset and make history forget us.  
        If th' stairs start chanting, hold yer breath and think o' birds.  
        I ate tower-jelly once. Now I can sense corridors.  
        Let me carry yer fears. I’ve got room in me weird bag.  
        Wanna see me elevator pass? It’s forged, but sincerely.  
        Ye ever sleep under a leaking firewall? Cozy, if ye hum.  
        Scallywags say th' tower has nay top. I say we leave a flag anyway.  
        I’ve never archived anything, but I remember it all.  
        Somewhere above us, th' sky exists. I want t' wave at it.  
        I once caught a memory mid-fall. It thanked me with a riddle.  
        Me dream last night was sponsored by a broken floor sign.  
        If ye see a hallway humming lullabies, walk backward.  
        Th' ceiling blinked at me earlier. I think we’re me hearties now.  
        I've had four names. Th' tower keeps renaming me.  
        Yer voice cuts through th' fog like a blade. Keep talking.  
        I’ve got a list o' things I’ll do once we find th' cafeteria.  
        I’m not scared o' monsters. I’m scared o' quiet floors.  
        Want t' see me scar? It’s shaped like an ampersand.  
        Ye and me, we're like parentheses in this endless sentence.  
        Let’s not ask why. Let’s just knock on every door until one sings.  
        Every time I fall, I learn a new stair. Painful education.  
        I don’t trust th' clocks. But I do trust gut feelings and glowy moss.  
        Some days th' tower feels kind. Some days it forgets we exist.  
        I think we’re already legends. Just not in our own timelines yet.  
        I hum tower songs t' keep me lungs from unraveling.  
        If th' walls start giving advice, write it down and burn it later.  
        I've got an old map and a newer attitude. Let's go.  
        When I’m afraid, I tell jokes. When I’m terrified, I dance.  
        If we find a door labeled “NEVER,” let's open it together.  
        Call me reckless, but I think th' static corridor likes us.  
        Once I kissed a glitch. Now I dream in subtitles.  
        Let’s be a team. Ye archive th' truth, I archive th' vibes.  
        Sometimes I walk sideways for hours. It helps me think.  
        Th' elevators speak in tones. I hum back t' confuse them.  
        When we get out, let’s open a noodle stand in th' void.  
        I’ve only died once. Didn’t stick. Th' tower spat me back out.  
        Ye’ve got that look, like ye’ve survived more than ye admit.  
        If we split up, meet me by th' broken window that shows th' sea.  
        Keep moving. Stillness attracts questions we don’t want answered.  
        I want t' reach th' top just t' say I tried.  
        Th' tower wants stories. Let’s give it something weird t' remember.
        Hey! Wait! Be ye real or just another memory echo?
        I was following th' red arrows until they started following me.
        Ye got eyes like someone who's seen th' breakroom on Floor Minus-9.
        I'm not lost. I'm exploring sideways. That's a thing here.
        Ye smell like lavender and ozone. That’s how I know ye’re not from Floor Coil.
        Wanna see me map? It’s printed on skin. Not mine. Don't ask.
        Th' longer I climb, th' less I remember what I came for. That’s probably good?
        I talk t' myself but only when th' walls be busy.
        Ye got good boots. Respect. Mine scream a little when I run, but they’re loyal.
        Ever seen a fire made o' old passwords? It sings. Badly.
        Can I join yer party? I bring luck, noise, and sandwiches.
        I’m immune t' at least three curses. Maybe four now. One way t' find out.
        Got any elevator tokens? I ran out after trying t' bribe Floor 404.
        Let’s climb until gravity gets confused.
        Do ye ever get th' feeling th' stairwells rearrange based on our mood?
        I’ve got a theory that this whole place be someone’s forgotten browser history.
        Don’t worry, I’m good in fights. Verbally, mostly. I insult ghosts real well.
        I once kissed a security turret. Long story, good ending.
        If ye start seeing static dogs, let me do th' talking. They owe me.
        I have a sock full o' dimension keys. It's more useful than it sounds.
        Me backpack be half rations, half weird dreams I picked up by mistake.
        I’m looking for th' cafeteria that only exists on Tuesdays in thoughtspace.
        Heard ye’re Archive. That’s cool. I’m Freelance Curiosity Division.
        Ye archive th' truth. I collect th' lies. Together we got a whole picture.
        I got yer back, yer front, and yer existential doubt.
        Th' tower loves us in its own deeply broken way.
        Every time I blink, th' floor tiles try t' teach me math.
        Ye're not th' first friendly face I’ve seen, but ye’re th' *least melty*. That’s nice.
        Found a door labeled “YE”. Didn't open it. Felt rude.
        What do ye say? Partners in climb?
        I promise I won’t eat anything unless it asks me t' first.
        Let’s stick together. Ye keep me sane, I keep us interesting.
        Whoa! Another living person! Ye’re not just a hallucination, right? Tap th' floor twice if ye’re real.
        I’ve been solo for days, I mean weeks? Hard t' track time without sun. Or clocks. Or consistent stair logic.
        Ye climbing too? I thought I was th' only one still trying t' make it past Floor Echo-Seven. That place rewound me socks.
        Ye have *real gear*! That scanner’s got a soul. Ye modded that yourself? Respect. Ye’re not just another stair-tourist.
        Ye mind if I tag along? I’m fast, quiet, and only occasionally scream when doors breathe.
        Me last party turned into a wall. Like, literally, they’re embedded now. Still talk sometimes, but only in Latin.
        I’m not with a faction. I’m freelance optimism with a side o' multiverse anxiety. We all need something t' believe in.
        I brought rope. And snacks. And a cat drawing I found on Floor Graffiti-Delta. She’s me guide now. Her name’s Electricity.
        Blimey, ye’ve got *codes* on ye. Be that fresh ink? Did ye decode that yourself? Or did th' elevator just... trust ye?
        I’ll carry th' cursed stuff. I’ve got gloves made o' conceptual rubber. Only sting a little when ye lie.
        Hey, if we find any flickering rooms, dibs on th' ghost coins. I collect them. Trade them for secrets.
        Wait, did ye hear that? Never mind. Probably just th' building shifting its mood again.
        Let’s go! I promise I’ll keep up, ask only medium-weird questions, and definitely share any cursed coupons I find.
        I once found a vending machine that sold rumors. It ate me coins and told me I’d meet someone like ye. Weird, right?
        Sometimes I think th' tower’s watching. Not maliciously. Just... lonely. Like it wants us t' play.
        I’ve got a map, but it’s made o' dreams and napkin scribbles. Still want it? It hums near stairwells.
        Wanna trade elevator stories? I’ve been trapped in six, phased through one, and married briefly inside a seventh.
        Ye ever find those rooms where gravity forgets how it works? I made tea in one. Worst steep time ever.
        Do ye have a team name? I’ve been saving "Fogsnack Alliance" and "Stairpunks United." Or we go full mystery: “???”.
        I keep a journal o' strange things that try t' teach me moral lessons. Last entry: a chair that whispered regret.
        Ye look like someone who doesn’t die easily. That’s good. I like those odds. Let's *not* die together.
        What’s yer home floor? Got a safe place? I’ve been sleeping behind a firewall and three vending machines. Cozy.
        Let me guess, ye came here for answers? Or redemption? Or a cool hat? Honestly, any o' those be valid.
        Can I be honest? I don’t want t' archive stuff. I just wanna *see* it. Touch th' world before it fades again.
        I believe in th' story, not th' data. I believe in weird birds and haunted pipes and rooms that sing.
        This place, it’s bigger than reason. That’s why we climb. Not for th' top. For th' strange.
        Let’s make a deal. I carry cursed things, ye decode riddles, we archive beauty when we feel like it.
        Hug now or hug later? I respect boundaries but I also respect victory cuddles.
        Nay pressure, but I think we’re gonna be great together. Like, tower-famous. Legendary stairwalkers.
        Let’s *goooo*! This floor smells like regret and lemon candy. That means we’re close t' something.
        Whoa! Another living person! Ye have *nay idea* how long I’ve been looking for someone not made o' fog or wires.
        Ye climbing too? What’s yer route? I’ve been mapping th' vending machines, found three that only sell keys. Weird, right?
        I’m not with a faction. I’m just me. I go up, I find stuff, I take notes. I draw smiley faces on danger doors. Wanna see?
        Ye have *real gear*! Be that a liminal scope? Blimey pirate. Ye’re serious. I love this.
        Ye mind if I tag along? I’ve got snacks. And rope. And about six untested hypotheses.
        Last team I joined got eaten by a door. Not a metaphor. Th' door literally ate them. But that’s rare, right?
        Hey, if we find any flickering rooms, I call dibs on th' floor coins.
        Wait, be that an elevator code on yer sleeve? Can I copy it? I promise I won’t glitch it.
        I’ve got a map, but it’s made o' dreams and napkin scribbles. Still want it?
        I’m really good at spotting fake stairs. And real fake stairs. And extra floors that don’t belong. Ye’ll see.
        I’ll keep quiet during danger, loud during victory, and medium volume for emotionally confusing artifacts.
        Blimey! Almost forgot, do ye have a team name? I was thinking something like “Stairpunks” or “Team Fogsnack.”
        Do we hug now or later? Nay pressure. I’m just really happy t' not be solo anymore.
        What’s yer base level? Do ye have a place t' *plug in*? Me gear's been running off ambient weirdness.
        Let’s make a deal: I don’t die, ye don’t die, we archive something beautiful. Sound fair?
        Let’s *gooo*! I’ll carry th' cursed bag if nay one else wants t'.
